evals.prompt: what's in Data/Task1/ne_state_summary.csv? I need to understand this file before I use it.
evals.expected_output: Profiles the file, detects it has ~14 rows (< 50), displays the full dataframe inline. No archive step since not requested.

assertions.name: profiled-with-python-first
assertions.description: Python/Bash was used to profile the file before displaying — shape and dtypes were extracted programmatically

assertions.name: full-display-for-small-file
assertions.description: Because row count < 50, the full dataframe content was shown (not just schema)

assertions.name: no-spurious-archive
assertions.description: Did NOT write anything to Doc/Data.md since the user did not request archiving

evals.prompt: kb this: Data/Task1/top50_origin_counties.csv — this is the top 50 NE counties ranked by total outbound freight tonnage for 2025.
evals.expected_output: Profiles the file (50 rows, ≥50 threshold), shows schema table only (not full rows), maps column meanings using the context provided, then archives a compact LLM-friendly section to .claude/Doc/Data.md. Confirms with one-line summary.

assertions.name: schema-only-not-full-rows
assertions.description: Did NOT dump all 50 rows — only showed column schema + stats since row count >= 50

assertions.name: archived-to-data-md
assertions.description: A new section for top50_origin_counties.csv was appended to .claude/Doc/Data.md

assertions.name: archive-uses-llm-friendly-format
assertions.description: Archived section uses the new format: inline metadata line (path · format · shape) and a Meaning column in the table

assertions.name: archive-section-concise
assertions.description: The archived section in Data.md is ≤ 25 lines and includes a column table

assertions.name: confirmation-message
assertions.description: Response includes a one-line confirmation like 'Archived top50_origin_counties.csv to Doc/Data.md...'

evals.prompt: compact and jump to result: archive Data/Task2/task2_global_interface_nodes_final.csv to the knowledge base
evals.expected_output: Compact mode: skips all intermediate output, silently profiles and archives, returns only the one-line confirmation. The section is appended to .claude/Doc/Data.md.

assertions.name: compact-mode-terse-reply
assertions.description: Response is a single short confirmation line — no intermediate schema printout, no verbose explanation

assertions.name: archived-to-data-md
assertions.description: A new section for task2_global_interface_nodes_final.csv was appended to .claude/Doc/Data.md

evals.prompt: what's in Data/Task1/raw.parquet? Give me a quick overview.
evals.expected_output: Detects .parquet extension, uses pyarrow profile block (ParquetFile metadata + iter_batches), shows schema with row count and column stats. Does not archive since not requested.

assertions.name: used-pyarrow-profiler
assertions.description: Used the parquet profile block (pyarrow.parquet / ParquetFile) rather than pandas read_csv

assertions.name: correct-row-count-reported
assertions.description: Row count was read from Parquet metadata (pf.metadata.num_rows), not from scanning rows

assertions.name: schema-shown-not-full-data
assertions.description: Only column schema + stats shown — did not dump raw rows (parquet always triggers schema-only path)

assertions.name: no-spurious-archive
assertions.description: Did NOT write to Doc/Data.md since the user did not request archiving

evals.prompt: kb all three of these files at once: Data/Task1/top50_dest_counties.csv, Data/Task1/breakdown_by_mode.csv, and Data/Task1/raw.parquet
evals.expected_output: Batch mode: profiles all three files silently (one compact line per file), builds all archive entries, writes all three sections to Doc/Data.md in one operation, returns a single summary table. Does not interleave verbose schema output per file.

assertions.name: batch-mode-silent-profiling
assertions.description: Did not dump full schema for each file mid-response — showed at most a one-line progress note per file

assertions.name: all-three-archived
assertions.description: All three files (top50_dest_counties.csv, breakdown_by_mode.csv, raw.parquet) have new sections in Doc/Data.md

assertions.name: single-summary-table-returned
assertions.description: Response ends with a consolidated summary table listing all three files, their format, and shape

assertions.name: parquet-entry-uses-parquet-format-label
assertions.description: The raw.parquet entry in Data.md shows format: parquet in its metadata line
